# Scenarios

- [Brunswick resident - a self-administered city](brunswick-resident-wrong-agency.md) - Fact pattern - Brunswick is not a RITA member; its own tax department handles Brunswick taxes, while any RITA-member workplace claim still goes to RITA.
- [RITA suburb resident, Cleveland office - the two-agency case](cleveland-office-cca-boundary.md) - Fact pattern - Cleveland's tax is administered by CCA, not RITA, so a refund of Cleveland withholding and its residence-side effect run through two different agencies.
- [Under-18 with a summer job - the age exemption](under-18-summer-job.md) - Fact pattern - municipal tax withheld from an under-18 worker's wages. RITA's stated rule from tax year 2024 and the Form 10A claim reason that applies.
- [Work-from-home in 2020 or 2021 - the closed years](wfh-2020-2021-closed-years.md) - Fact pattern - remote work during the pandemic years. What the record shows - HB 197 (upheld in Schaad), HB 110, and claim windows that closed in 2024 and 2025.
- [Hybrid worker, 2024 - Medina resident, Solon office](wfh-2024-medina-resident-solon-office.md) - Fact pattern - employer withheld Solon tax all year while the employee worked some days at home in Medina. Which rules, forms, and documents apply.
